channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Hormuz Shipping Activity Accelerates
item.description: A growing stream of stranded oil is making its way out of the Strait of Hormuz while empty Iranian vessels rush in, as the U.S.-Iran interim peace deal sparks one of the biggest days of activity since the conflict began.

item.title: ExxonMobil Backs 1st South African LNG Import Terminal with Supply Deal
item.description: Zululand Energy Terminal, being developed as South Africa's first receiving terminal for liquefied natural gas, announced a preliminary agreement for ExxonMobil to supply LNG to the facility.

item.title: Oil prices hit lowest level since March after US-Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: Oil prices dropped to their lowest point since early March on 18 June, following the announcement of a US-Iran interim agreement aimed at ending the conflict and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Azerbaijan Moves Into Uzbekistan’s Fast-Growing Mining Sector
item.description: Azerbaijan and Uzbekistan are strengthening economic connections, signing a variety of agreements covering mining, banking and tourism. Uzbek Prime Minister Abdulla Aripov and his Azerbaijani counterpart, Ali Asadov…

item.title: Sable Seeks $1B Loan
item.description: The owner of the Santa Ynez Unit in California launched a marketing process for a senior secured term loan with a principal amount of up to $1 billion.

item.title: Oil Prices Are Crashing. Inventories Tell A Different Story.
item.description: Oil traders have gone from pricing in the worst supply disruption in modern history to pricing in a recovery that hasn't happened yet. Inventory data suggests they’re getting ahead of themselves. Brent crude fell below…

item.title: Equinor Targets 150,000 Boed Production Growth by 2030
item.description: The majority state-owned energy major plans 6-8 tieback projects offshore Norway each year.

item.title: Sweden Picks Rolls-Royce for First New Reactor Since the 1980s
item.description: Rolls-Royce SMR just added its third major European contract. Videberg Kraft, backed by Sweden’s utility Vattenfall, selected the UK design for three units on the west coast, making it Sweden’s first new nuclear plant…

item.title: High Tanker Rates Disrupt Persian Gulf Oil Shipments to Asia
item.description: The biggest state-owned refiners in China and India have failed to procure supertankers to load crude from the Persian Gulf later in June as tanker rates are too high and guarantees on safe passage through the Strait of…

item.title: The AI Spending Boom Is Creating a Depreciation Time Bomb
item.description: The eye-watering capital expenditure plans of Big Tech has been one of the year’s biggest stories. Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft have all splurged to secure a podium spot in the race to build out the…

item.title: India's Pump Prices Stuck Even As Global Crude Crashes
item.description: India’s fuel prices will remain elevated for some time despite the declining international crude prices, according to India’s Union Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Suresh Gopi. Indian drivers will not…

item.title: Why the Moment for a Deal Could Not Be Better for Iran
item.description: Iran does not need a deal right now because its military or political room for manoeuvre has narrowed. It needs one because the crisis has turned oil (Tehran’s most important economic weapon) into a problematic asset…

item.title: ExxonMobil and ZET strike deal for South Africa’s first LNG terminal
item.description: The Zululand Energy Terminal (ZET) has executed a heads of agreement (HoA) with ExxonMobil South Africa LNG, an affiliate of ExxonMobil, to establish South Africa’s first liquefied natural gas (LNG) import terminal.

item.title: EnBW secures additional US LNG volumes from Venture Global
item.description: German energy company EnBW has secured additional US liquefied natural gas (LNG) volumes from Venture Global under new binding agreements for about 0.82 million tonnes per annum (mtpa).

item.title: JPMorgan: European Stocks Are Attractively Cheap After Oil Price Slump
item.description: Europe’s relatively cheap stocks could become attractive for investors as the oil prices plunged and markets hope the Hormuz crisis is over, according to JPMorgan. Assuming that the Middle East crisis has peaked and oil…

item.title: Who Would Take Iran’s Uranium?
item.description: As negotiations over Iran's nuclear program continue, the fate of Tehran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium remains one of the most difficult issues to resolve. Before US and Israeli air strikes on Iranian nuclear…

item.title: Refinery Hit as Ukraine Pounds Moscow
item.description: Russia's capital faced a record air bombardment overnight, with drones reaching the Moscow Oil Refinery, disrupting airport operations and forcing the closure of several major roads in and around the city.

item.title: Eskom Reports Sustained Improvements in Grid Reliability
item.description: The South African state-owned utility continues to register improvements in electricity supply reliability as winter demand escalates, reporting nearly 400 consecutive days without power interruptions from mid-May.

item.title: Australia’s Renewable Project Pipeline Is Booming
item.description: Following government tenders for nearly 10 gigawatts of new renewable energy capacity, Australia’s pipeline of probable clean energy projects jumped by about 30% to as much as 32.3 GW in the biggest surge on record. The…

item.title: US, Iran Peace Pact Takes Effect
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, speeding up the timeline for the agreement to go into effect despite blowback from Republicans who said it amounted…
